name: Ila 28
description_short: The NRA-ILA is the legislative advocacy and education division of the National Rifle Association, not a consumer finance or payday alternative company.
description_long: The National Rifle Association's Institute for Legislative Action (NRA-ILA) is a non-profit organization dedicated to Second Amendment advocacy, gun rights legislation, and firearms safety education. Founded as part of the broader NRA mission, the ILA focuses on political action, policy advocacy, and legal education related to firearms ownership and use. The organization provides resources on federal and state gun laws, candidate ratings, voter registration assistance, and legislative tracking for gun-related policy. Members can access information about firearms training, safety programs, hunting education, competitive shooting, and related outdoor activities. The NRA-ILA distinguishes itself through its comprehensive legal resources, including guides to NFA-regulated firearms, state-by-state gun law databases, and BATFE reference materials. It operates as an educational and advocacy platform rather than a financial services provider. This organization has been fundamentally miscategorized as a "payday-alternatives" lender. The NRA-ILA provides no financial products, loans, credit services, or consumer finance solutions whatsoever. It is purely an advocacy, education, and membership organization focused on Second Amendment rights and firearms-related activities.
services: State and federal gun law databases and BATFE regulations reference
Firearm safety training and NRA instructor certification
Hunter education programs and youth shooting sports
Voter registration and candidate ratings on gun policy
Legislative tracking and policy advocacy resources
Firearms training course catalog and NRA-ILA online education
Legal guidance on NFA-regulated firearms and suppressors
Range safety officer and shooting sports coach training
Adaptive shooting programs for disabled shooters
pros: Comprehensive, free public access to state and federal gun law databases and BATFE regulations
Extensive firearms safety and training resources including Eddie Eagle youth programs and instructor certification
Non-partisan voter registration and candidate rating tools for gun policy issues
Hunter education programs and youth shooting sports development opportunities
Free legal guidance materials on NFA-regulated firearms and transportation with firearms
cons: Not a financial services provider—cannot assist with loans, credit, or emergency cash needs
Requires membership for some benefits and full access to certain resources
Focused exclusively on gun rights advocacy—no general consumer finance assistance
Website navigation is complex and oriented toward advocacy rather than consumer services
best_for: Gun owners seeking state and federal firearms law information
Individuals interested in firearm safety training and education
Hunters and competitive shooters looking for education and community
diagnosis: This organization is fundamentally miscategorized and should not be listed as a payday-alternative lender or any consumer finance product. It is a non-profit advocacy and education organization focused exclusively on Second Amendment rights and firearms-related activities, providing no financial services, loans, or credit products of any kind.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
